citations.claim: The denied service is semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection.
citations.verbatim_quote: your prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection has been DENIED

citations.claim: The denial rationale asserts the member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy.
citations.verbatim_quote: Member does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Jane A. Doe (member ID BS-A1234567, date of birth May 14, 1978), enrolled in BlueSun Health Premium HMO, to formally appeal the April 1, 2026 denial of her prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection. The denial letter states that the request has been denied on the grounds that the member purportedly does not meet all criteria for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy. As set forth below, the medical record demonstrates that Ms. Doe meets each applicable medical necessity criterion under policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3, and the denial should be overturned.

citations.claim: Policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 requires a BMI at or above 30 kg/m^2.
citations.verbatim_quote: Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2

citations.claim: The patient's recorded BMI is 34.2, above the policy threshold.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: BMI threshold
paragraphs.text: Policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 requires a Body Mass Index (BMI) at or above 30 kg/m^2. Ms. Doe's most recent progress note, dated February 15, 2026, documents a BMI of 34.2, which exceeds the policy threshold by more than four points. This is also consistent with the American Diabetes Association 2024 Standards of Care, which recommend pharmacologic weight-management therapy for adults with BMI >= 30.

citations.claim: Policy requires a supervised diet, exercise, and behavior-modification program of at least six months.
citations.verbatim_quote: supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months

citations.claim: The patient has documented participation in a supervised Weight Watchers program exceeding six months.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Supervised diet and exercise program
paragraphs.text: Policy MEDPOL-GLP1-v3 further requires documented participation in a supervised program of reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity, and behavior modification for a minimum of six months. Ms. Doe has exceeded this requirement: per the November 20, 2025 progress note, she has attended 21 of 24 scheduled Weight Watchers group sessions — a structured, supervised program of diet, activity, and behavior modification spanning approximately ten months, well beyond the six-month minimum. Her sustained engagement despite inadequate weight reduction supports the addition of pharmacologic therapy.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: The medical record establishes that Ms. Doe satisfies every applicable criterion under MEDPOL-GLP1-v3: her BMI of 34.2 exceeds the 30 kg/m^2 threshold, and her participation in a supervised Weight Watchers program for approximately ten months exceeds the six-month supervised diet-and-exercise requirement. The stated basis for denial — that she does not meet all criteria — is not supported by the chart. I therefore respectfully request that BlueSun Health Premium HMO overturn the denial and approve semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection for this patient. I am available to discuss this case further and to provide any additional documentation required.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — semaglutide (Ozempic) 1mg weekly subcutaneous injection — member BS-A1234567
